sections.paragraph:
ἀπόδεκτος, ον (s. ἀποδέχομαι) is accented thus almost exclusively in the NT tradition (but s. CGregory, Prolegomena to Tdf. NT8 1894, 100f), but ἀποδεκτός elsewhere (Plut., Mor. 1061a; Sext. Emp., Math. 11, 83; so also OGI 441, 100 ἀποδεκ[τὰ ὑπάρχει]ν δεῖν). Strictly speaking, ἀποδεκτός means acceptable, welcome and ἀπόδεκτος pleasing (W-S. §6, 4; Mlt-H. 58). The former is given Dg 8:3 (cp. Just., A I, 43, 2 οὔθʼ οὗτος ἀπόδεκτος οὐδὲ ἐκεῖνος μεμπτέος); the latter has been given preference in 1 Ti 2:3; 5:4, but the accentuation ἀποδεκτός for these pass. in the sense be approved, find approval deserves review, esp. when the administrative aspect of the next entry is considered in relation to the official-sounding tone of the Pauline pass.—M-M. TW.

NT NT = New Testament

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

esp. esp. = especially

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 109
